claim: On July 24, 2024, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu addressed a joint meeting of the U.S. Congress. He was received with a roughly eight-minute standing ovation on arrival and, by the count of multiple outlets, between 39 and 79 applause-or-standing-ovation interruptions during the speech itself — with one of the more widely cited counts placing standing ovations specifically at 58. The address was boycotted by 92 U.S. lawmakers (89 Democrats, 2 Independents, and 1 Republican). The speech took place during ongoing U.S.-supplied Israeli military operations in Gaza.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “2024 visit by Benjamin Netanyahu to the United States”
sources.quote: 2024 visit by Benjamin Netanyahu to the United States

sources.label: NPR — “Netanyahu met with standing ovation and protest over joint meeting of Congress” (Jul 24, 2024)
Al Jazeera — “Key takeaways from Netanyahu's speech and the protests outside US Congress”
The Times of Israel — “Netanyahu gets minute-long standing ovation as he arrives in Congress”
Wilson Center — “360° Reactions: Netanyahu's Address to the US Congress”
Stelle.World / @stelle.world, Instagram reel DVtM1wwESCr (Mar 10, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: The reel cites '26 standing ovations'; the actual count varies by source from 39 (CNN/AP) to 79 total applause moments (with 58 standing ovations specifically per Al Jazeera). All published counts are higher than the figure cited in the reel. The reception was historic — Netanyahu is one of only a handful of foreign leaders to have addressed a joint session of Congress four times.
